behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff: reject path must both return 401 and suppress enqueue. A unit test of only HMAC comparison misses forwarding side effects. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; in-process HTTP + wired modules can expose this bug.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler with real signature-check middleware and a spy/fake queue adapter in-process. Send a request with mismatched body/signature, assert 401, and assert enqueue was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicit cross-module ordering (queue write must precede HTTP ack). Unit is too low because ordering bug appears at module boundary. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required by text; ordering can be verified with in-process server plus real queue module (local persistence backend).
behaviors.test_description: Start handler in-process with real queue implementation (or real local persistence layer). Instrument/write timestamp or hook points, send valid webhook, assert queue write committed before response completion.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: This is async decoupling between request path and downstream processor modules. Unit test of a single function can miss wiring where await is accidentally introduced. Rejected System because behavior does not require real external infra; controlled in-process delayed processor is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream worker in-process. Make worker intentionally slow/blocking, send valid event, assert HTTP response returns before worker completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real wall-clock latency under concurrency and event-loop/runtime behavior; mocks can hide queue contention/scheduling delays. This crosses into real process/timing infrastructure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior has no AI/ML decision steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service as a real process with HTTP endpoint. Create concurrent load where prior events trigger slow downstream work, then measure end-to-end response latency for new webhook requests and assert p95 (or strict per-request bound) under 3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Duplicate suppression is state handoff across handler + dedupe/persistence path; bug appears when modules interact over stored IDs. Unit is too low for race/state integration defects. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Using handler plus real dedupe store/queue wiring, send two requests with same event ID and valid signatures. Assert first write succeeds and second is ignored (no second queue record).

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says 'queue reports it is full'—this is translation of downstream status to HTTP response. Integration is needed to verify module boundary mapping. Rejected System because this behavior is not about proving real capacity mechanics (that is behavior 7).
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue interface implementation/fake that returns 'full'. Send valid request, assert 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After header, and assert no downstream processing start.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates queue component plus persistence backend interaction (stateful enforcement), not just a pure function. Unit risks missing persistence-level enforcement defects. Rejected System because behavior does not require real external network/subprocess; local persistent backend integration is sufficient unless implementation uses external queue service.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with a small configured capacity against its real local storage backend. Enqueue up to limit, then attempt one more and assert rejection/error and unchanged stored depth.
